strategic_goals: Priority I: Market-responsive portfolio management that continuously adapts to workforce demands
Priority II: Skills-based architecture that bridges learning experiences, skills, and prior learning credit
Priority III: Strengthened and diversified learner population to expand access and serve historically underserved populations
Priority IV: Streamlined, learner-centric ecosystem with automated and tailored support
Priority V: Intentional study of and investment in the needs of our community to ensure agency and support for all employees
strategic_plan_theme: Innovation and transformation to bridge industry skill and knowledge gaps while preparing students for the jobs of today and the future
vision: UMGC's mission is to 'inspire hope, empower dreams, and transform lives . . . one student at a time.' We are an open enrollment Maryland state university dedicated to serving working adults, military servicemembers and their families, and veterans who reside in Maryland, across the United States, and around the world. Founded in 1947, UMGC has grown to become the largest public university in the nation, offering affordable, skills-based online programs and comprehensive student services to help learners advance their education and plan for their next success, no matter where life takes them.
mission: Inspire hope, empower dreams, and transform lives . . . one student at a time
description: University of Maryland Global Campus (UMGC) is an innovative, transformational university that bridges industry skill and knowledge gaps while preparing students for the jobs of today and the future. With 75+ years of experience serving working adults, servicemembers, and their families, UMGC understands the unique academic journey that entails hard work, perseverance, and determination.
unique_value_propositions: Affordability - Competitive tuition rates with multiple payment and finance options
Convenience - Flexible online and hybrid programs that fit into busy schedules
Flexibility - Multiple start dates with streamlined admission process
Accessibility - Open enrollment policy welcoming diverse learners
Skills-based education - Programs designed to equip students with key skills in communication, leadership, and critical thinking
No-cost digital course materials - Most programs use digital materials instead of expensive textbooks
Lifetime career services - Comprehensive career support from acceptance through alumni status
Military-focused - Dedicated military advising team and worldwide locations at military installations
Global reach - 175+ classroom and service locations worldwide including U.S., Europe, and Asia divisions
State university reputation - Backed by the University System of Maryland
target_learners: Working adults balancing career and education

priority_i.title: Market-responsive portfolio management that continuously adapts to workforce demands
priority_i.focus: Ensuring academic programs align with current and emerging workforce needs, continuously adapting curriculum to meet industry demands
key_initiatives: Regularly assess market trends to inform program development and portfolio decisions
Engage with industry partners to ensure curriculum relevance and alignment with workforce needs
Implement agile processes for program updates and new program creation
Develop strategic partnerships with organizations like Guild and Coursera to expand learning pathways
Redesign academic programs (e.g., MBA) to be more flexible, customized, and affordable
Continuously monitor employer needs and workforce demands to adapt offerings
success_metrics: Program alignment with current and emerging job market demands
Employer satisfaction with graduate preparedness
Graduate employment rates and career advancement
Enrollment growth in market-responsive programs
Partnership outcomes and effectiveness
Program completion rates and credential attainment

priority_ii.title: Skills-based architecture that bridges learning experiences, skills, and prior learning credit
priority_ii.focus: Creating a comprehensive system that recognizes and credits prior learning, connects skills to credentials, and provides clear pathways for learners
key_initiatives: Map curricula to industry-recognized skills and certifications
Provide learners with clear pathways to career advancement through skills-based credentials
Expand credit for prior learning assessment and recognition
Develop competency-based pathways that connect learning experiences to workforce competencies
Collaborate with employers to validate skill frameworks and credentialing
Integrate experiential learning opportunities to build practical, applicable skills
Create stackable credentials that allow learners to build toward degrees incrementally
success_metrics: Credit for prior learning awarded to learners
Skills-to-credential pathways created and utilized
Learner time-to-degree reduction through prior learning credit
Number of industry-recognized certifications integrated into programs
Employer validation of skill frameworks
Learner progression through stackable credential pathways

priority_iii.title: Strengthened and diversified learner population to expand access and serve historically underserved populations
priority_iii.focus: Expanding access to education for diverse populations, including historically underserved communities, and supporting their success
key_initiatives: Identify and engage underserved learner demographics through targeted outreach
Expand scholarship and financial aid programs to reduce barriers
Enhance support services tailored to diverse learner needs
Develop programs specifically tailored to military and veteran students
Expand global partnerships to reach international learners
Implement diversity, equity, and inclusion training for staff working with learners
Create strategic partnerships to create new learning pathways for diverse populations
success_metrics: Enrollment diversity metrics and representation of underserved populations
Retention rates by population and demographic groups
Graduation rates for historically underserved populations
Enrollment growth among target demographics
Scholarship and financial aid utilization rates
Learner satisfaction scores across diverse populations

priority_iv.title: Streamlined, learner-centric ecosystem with automated and tailored support
priority_iv.focus: Creating an efficient, user-friendly experience with automated processes and personalized support throughout the learner journey
key_initiatives: Develop integrated reporting system offering comprehensive view of learners for personalized, data-driven discussions
Create centralized data hub integrating systems used by team members working directly with learners
Refine early alert capability to identify at-risk learners promptly
Implement personalized learning pathways based on learner goals and needs
Develop regular reporting and dashboards tailored to roles and responsibilities of teams interacting with learners
Enhance support services with automated and tailored interventions
Leverage digital technologies to improve educational delivery and administrative processes
Implement responsive, tailored, and seamless learner experience across all touchpoints
success_metrics: Process automation rates and reduction in manual processes
Learner satisfaction scores and experience ratings
Time-to-enrollment reduction and streamlined processes
Support response times and resolution rates
Early alert effectiveness in identifying and supporting at-risk learners
Data hub utilization and integration success
Personalized pathway adoption and completion rates

priority_v.title: Intentional study of and investment in the needs of our community to ensure agency and support for all employees
priority_v.focus: Supporting faculty and staff success through intentional investment in their needs, professional development, and workplace satisfaction
key_initiatives: Conduct regular assessments of staff and faculty needs through engagement surveys and feedback mechanisms
Provide professional development opportunities aligned with strategic goals and individual growth
Promote culture of collaboration and continuous improvement
Ensure resources and support systems are in place for team success
Invest in workplace culture initiatives that foster innovation and excellence
Review and enhance compensation and benefits to support employee well-being
Create pathways for career advancement and professional growth
Foster spirit of innovation and digital transformation capabilities among staff
success_metrics: Employee satisfaction scores and engagement metrics
Staff and faculty retention rates
Professional development participation and completion rates
Workplace culture metrics and climate surveys
Employee agency and support perception scores
Innovation and collaboration indicators
Professional growth and advancement rates

strategic_enablers.name: Technology Infrastructure and Digital Transformation
strategic_enablers.description: Robust technology infrastructure and digital transformation capabilities enable market-responsive portfolio management, streamlined learner experiences, and skills-based architecture. Digital platforms support automated processes, personalized pathways, and integrated systems that enhance operational efficiency and learner success.

strategic_enablers.name: Data and Analytics Capabilities
strategic_enablers.description: Comprehensive data hub and analytics capabilities enable data-driven decision making across all priorities. Integrated reporting systems, early alert capabilities, and personalized dashboards support market analysis, learner success interventions, and strategic portfolio management.

strategic_enablers.name: Strategic Partnerships and Collaborations
strategic_enablers.description: Strategic partnerships with organizations like Guild, Coursera, employers, and community organizations enable market-responsive program development, expanded learning pathways, and alignment with workforce needs. Partnerships support targeted expansion and skills-based credentialing.

strategic_enablers.name: People and Culture
strategic_enablers.description: Investment in people through professional development, supportive culture, and collaborative environment enables innovation, excellence, and effective execution of all strategic priorities. Engaged and skilled workforce drives learner success and organizational transformation.

strategic_values: Learner Centricity - Prioritizing the needs and success of learners in all decisions and actions
Innovation - Embracing new ideas and technologies to enhance learning experiences and operational efficiency
Integrity - Upholding ethical standards, honesty, and transparency in all activities
Diversity and Inclusion - Fostering a diverse and inclusive community that respects and values diverse perspectives
Collaboration - Working together across departments and with external partners to achieve common goals
Excellence - Striving for the highest quality in educational programs, services, and operations
Accessibility - Ensuring education is within reach for all individuals regardless of background or circumstances
Affordability - Offering cost-effective educational programs to minimize financial barriers for learners
Respect - Treating all individuals with dignity and consideration
Accountability - Taking responsibility for actions and outcomes, ensuring transparency and trust

foundational_commitments.learner_centricity: UMGC is dedicated to placing learners at the center of its mission, ensuring that educational experiences are tailored to meet their diverse needs and aspirations
foundational_commitments.our_people: The university values its community, recognizing that the collective efforts of its teams drive success and innovation
foundational_commitments.state_of_maryland: UMGC is committed to serving the state of Maryland by providing accessible and affordable education to its residents
foundational_commitments.global_reach: With a presence in over 175 locations worldwide, UMGC aims to meet learners wherever they are, offering flexible and quality education
foundational_commitments.military: The university has a longstanding commitment to military-affiliated students, providing tailored programs and support services to meet their unique needs
foundational_commitments.spirit_of_innovation: UMGC embraces a culture of innovation, continuously seeking new ways to enhance learning experiences and operational efficiency
foundational_commitments.digital_transformation: The university leverages digital technologies to improve educational delivery and administrative processes, ensuring a seamless experience for learners

strategic_challenges.challenge: Rapidly changing workforce demands and evolving skill requirements
strategic_challenges.impact: Academic programs may become misaligned with employer needs, reducing graduate employability and program relevance. Requires continuous monitoring and agile adaptation of curriculum and offerings.
strategic_challenges.mitigation: Priority I (Market-responsive portfolio management) addresses this through regular market trend assessment, industry partnerships, and agile program development processes

strategic_challenges.challenge: Technology infrastructure and integration needs for streamlined learner experience
strategic_challenges.impact: Fragmented systems create inefficiencies, poor learner experience, and limit data-driven decision making. Manual processes slow down operations and reduce responsiveness.
strategic_challenges.mitigation: Priority IV (Streamlined learner-centric ecosystem) addresses this through integrated reporting systems, data hub development, and digital transformation initiatives

strategic_challenges.challenge: Serving diverse and historically underserved populations while maintaining quality
strategic_challenges.impact: Traditional educational models may not meet needs of diverse learners, leading to lower retention and completion rates. Requires tailored approaches and enhanced support services.
strategic_challenges.mitigation: Priority III (Strengthened and diversified learner population) addresses this through targeted outreach, enhanced support services, and diversity/equity/inclusion training

strategic_challenges.challenge: Recognizing and crediting prior learning while maintaining academic rigor
strategic_challenges.impact: Learners with extensive experience may face barriers to degree completion if prior learning isn't recognized, increasing time and cost to completion.
strategic_challenges.mitigation: Priority II (Skills-based architecture) addresses this through expanded prior learning assessment, skills mapping, and competency-based pathways

strategic_challenges.challenge: Faculty and staff engagement and retention in competitive market
strategic_challenges.impact: High turnover and low engagement reduce institutional knowledge, innovation capacity, and quality of learner support. Impacts all strategic priorities.
strategic_challenges.mitigation: Priority V (Investment in our people) addresses this through needs assessment, professional development, culture initiatives, and resource support

strategic_opportunities.opportunity: Growing demand for skills-based education and stackable credentials
strategic_opportunities.potential_impact: UMGC can position itself as a leader in skills-based education by expanding credentialing pathways, prior learning recognition, and industry-aligned programs. This aligns with workforce needs and learner preferences for flexible, career-relevant education.

strategic_opportunities.opportunity: Expansion of online and hybrid learning post-pandemic
strategic_opportunities.potential_impact: UMGC's expertise in online education positions it to serve more learners globally. Enhanced digital platforms and personalized experiences can differentiate UMGC in the competitive online education market.

strategic_opportunities.opportunity: Strategic partnerships with employers and education platforms (Guild, Coursera)
strategic_opportunities.potential_impact: Partnerships create new learning pathways, expand reach to employer-sponsored learners, and provide opportunities for program innovation and market expansion.

strategic_opportunities.opportunity: Increasing focus on diversity, equity, and inclusion in higher education
strategic_opportunities.potential_impact: UMGC can strengthen its commitment to serving diverse populations, expand access to underserved communities, and build reputation as an inclusive institution that supports all learners.

strategic_opportunities.opportunity: Data-driven personalization and early intervention capabilities
strategic_opportunities.potential_impact: Advanced analytics and early alert systems enable proactive learner support, improving retention and success rates while demonstrating commitment to learner-centricity.

strategic_opportunities.opportunity: Military and veteran education benefits and support
strategic_opportunities.potential_impact: UMGC's longstanding commitment to military-affiliated students positions it to expand services and programs, leveraging its global presence and expertise in serving this population.
